risk_if_not_implemented: Without Coordination With External Providers, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Response Plan (IRP)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to maintain and make available a current and viable Incident Response Plan (IRP) to all stakeholders.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Coordination With External Providers (IRO-11.2) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Incident Reporting Assistance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to provide incident response advice and assistance to users of Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) for the handling and reporting of actual and potential cybersecurity and data protection incidents.
compensating_control_2.justification: Incident Reporting Assistance (IRO-11) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Coordination With External Providers (IRO-11.2) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
